news.summary: The latest report indicates a surge in COVID-19 cases, with a total of 5,000 new infections recorded in the past week. Authorities have confirmed that the current transmission rate stands at 1.5, suggesting a rapid spread of the virus. The total number of cases in the region has now reached 30,000, prompting concerns about the strain on healthcare facilities. Additionally, the death toll has risen to 500, highlighting the severity of the situation. Health officials are urging the public to adhere strictly to safety protocols to mitigate further spread of the virus.
news.headline: Chile's President Boric experiences all-time low approval rating as per latest report.

news.summary: A company based in Brecksville, Ohio has recently introduced two innovative suture passing solutions designed for accuracy and efficiency in minimally invasive surgeries. The products, RHAPSO™ and GEMINI®, aim to provide versatility and precision during procedures. The launch was announced on May 7, 2025, by Applied Medical Technology, Inc. RHAPSO™, known for its versatility...
news.headline: Applied Medical releases RHAPSO™ and GEMINI® suture passing instruments for minimally invasive procedures.

news.summary: In Pune, India on May 7, 2025, it was reported that the worldwide market for Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) Software was worth approximately USD 15,828.51 million in 2024. Projections indicate a significant growth as it is expected to reach approximately USD 62,175.53 million by the year 2032. This represents a strong Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 18.53% during the forecast period spanning from 2024 to 2032.
news.headline: Global VDI Software Market valued at USD 15,828.51 million in 2024 to peak at USD 62,175.53 million by 2032 with 18.53% CAGR.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, RBAZ Bancorp, Inc. confirmed the finalization of the transfer of most assets and liabilities of Republic Bank of Arizona to Pima Federal Credit Union on May 2, 2025. Following this Asset Sale, RBAZ ceased trading its common stock on the OTC Pink Market and concluded stock transfers on May 2, 2025. This shift marks a significant change in ownership and operations in the banking sector.
news.headline: RBAZ Bancorp dissolves, sells assets to Pima Federal Credit Union, delisting stock from OTC Market on May 2, 2025.

news.summary: In Houston, SynergenX, a prominent testosterone replacement therapy (TRT) provider for men, is collaborating with Low T Center to revolutionize hormone health by introducing a new promotion. Starting today, all SynergenX and Low T Center sites will provide complimentary consultations and testosterone tests. This campaign aims to encourage more men, especially those aged 3<x<, to proactively manage their health, eliminating the typical initial expenses that often surpass $100 in other facilities.
news.headline: Free testosterone testing and consultations launched by SynergenX and Low T Center campaign to empower more men.

news.summary: In a heartwarming event held at the prestigious Army Navy Country Club in Washington, the National Capitol Classic golf fundraiser was recently celebrated in support of veterans. The event, which took place on May 7, 2025, was co-hosted by SAIC and Amazon Web Services. Building Homes for Heroes, partnered with SAIC (NASDAQ) and AWS, organized the event, aiming to raise funds and awareness for veterans in need. The location, rich in history, provided a fitting backdrop for the occasion, highlighting the significance of supporting those who have served their country.
news.headline: Building Homes for Heroes to pay off mortgage for family of fallen Army soldier; fundraiser with SAIC and AWS.

news.summary: Shares of Barrick Gold Corp (NYSE: GOLD) are up as it announced first-quarter revenue of $3.13 billion, marking a 14% increase year over year (Y/Y), in line with expectations. The company's gold production saw a decline of 19% Y/Y to 758 thousand ounces, with gold sales at 751 thousand ounces, down 17% Y/Y. The average realized gold price increased by 40% Y/Y to $2,898 per ounce, while all-in sustaining costs (AISC) dropped by 20% Y/Y to $1,775 per ounce for the quarter. Additionally, copper production rose by 10% Y/Y to 44 thousand tonnes, but the average realized copper price fell by 17% Y/Y to $4.51 per ounce.
news.headline: Barrick's Q1 revenue is $3.13B, 758K gold oz produced (-19% Y/Y), 40% Y/Y rise in average gold price to $2,898/oz. Copper production surged 10%.

news.summary: The U.S. services sector experienced its slowest growth in over 17 months in April, with the Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) increasing from 50.8% in March to 51.6% in April, slightly above economists' expectations of 50.6%. However, this growth rate is the slowest since late 2023. The inflationary pressures surged, with the prices paid subindex jumping to 65.1% in April from 60.9% in March, the highest level since February 2023. These rising costs are attributed to trade tariff disruptions. The index for new orders also rose from 50.4% to 52.3%. The concerning combination of weakening demand and escalating costs due to tariffs could complicate the Federal Reserve's decisions going forward.
news.headline: U.S. services sector growth slows, inflation surges in April. Services PMI at 51.6%, prices paid up to 65.1%. New orders rise to 52.3%.

news.summary: Stocks of Netflix and movie studios have started to recover from losses following the threat of tariffs on foreign production. The issue arose after President Trump considered imposing taxes on foreign-made movies. This development has led to a decline in market value for these companies, with Netflix witnessing a significant drop in its stock prices. Investors are closely monitoring the situation to assess the potential impact of these tariffs on the industry.
news.headline: Stocks of Netflix and movie studios drop after Trump threatens tariffs on foreign-made movies. How will this impact?

news.summary: Investors are feeling cautious due to Fed Chairman Jerome Powell's concerns about the inflation impact of Trump's tariffs. The question arises whether Powell will adjust his firm stance on inflation during the Fed meeting. The S&P 500 has seen a rise amidst these uncertainties.
news.headline: Fed Chair may soften hawkish stance on Trump tariffs, worrying investors. S&P 500 up post meeting.

news.summary: Stock markets on Wall Street began the week cautiously, with major indices trading mostly flat by midday in New York. Investors reacted to new signs of inflation in the services sector, causing uncertainty about potential rate cuts and impacting risk tolerance. The ISM Services PMI for April indicated minimal growth, the slowest since late 2023, while the prices paid index surged to its highest level in over two years. This spike was attributed to increased input costs due to recent trade disruptions stemming from tariffs imposed by President Trump in the prior month. The S&P 500 saw a 0.2% decrease to 5,670, mirroring the Nasdaq 100 which also fell to 20,050. Conversely, the Dow Jones Industrial Average recorded a modest 0.2% increase. A higher close for the S&P 500 would mark the index's tenth consecutive session of gains, marking the lengthiest winning streak since September 1995. Equities faced pressure from rising bond yields, influencing the market sentiment.
news.headline: Stocks pause as Treasury yields rise, with S&P 500 slipping 0.2% to 5,670 and Nasdaq 100 matching the decline at 20,050.

news.summary: Following a surprising production increase by OPEC+, crude oil prices experienced a significant decline, leading to a downturn in U.S. energy stocks. Goldman Sachs responded by revising its oil price forecast, reducing it by $2 to $3. The adjusted projection anticipates average prices of $60 for Brent and $56 for WTI throughout the remainder of 2025, with estimates of $56 and $52 for 2026. Despite tight spot fundamentals, Goldman Sachs' commodity analyst Daan Struyven emphasized the bank's belief that the market's vulnerability to low oil prices is heightened by substantial spare capacity and recession risks. The selloff was triggered by OPEC+'s decision to raise production by 411,000 barrels per day. West Texas Intermediate futures began at $55.36 per barrel on Sunday in New York, recovering slightly to $57.10 early Monday.
news.headline: Goldman Sachs predicts $40 oil due to OPEC+ increase. Oil price forecast lowered, causing stock selloff. WTI opens at $55.36.

news.summary: In Dubai on May 7, 2025, Monetary Metals revealed a collaboration with ORO Labs in the UAE. This alliance introduces the first-ever blockchain gold token by ORO, offering a unique yield feature. This innovation offers a fresh avenue for Monetary Metals to expand its reach. Unlike other gold tokens focusing on custody services, ORO's token stands out for its yield-bearing capability. This collaboration signifies a significant development in the digital gold market, with the potential to revolutionize the sector.
news.headline: Monetary Metals partners with ORO Labs to launch first yield-bearing blockchain gold token, expanding distribution.
